payload.formal_model: Rubinstein alternating-offers: two players split a unit pie, alternating proposals each round, per-round discount factors delta_a, delta_b in (0,1). Unique SPE: agreement in round 1 at proposer share x* = (1-delta_b)/(1-delta_a*delta_b); more patient player (higher delta) gets more; as delta->1 the split approaches Nash bargaining. Ultimatum game is the one-round limit (delta_b=0 => x*=1), whose SPE (offer ~0) is empirically violated; behavioral layer replaces it with an offer distribution centered ~0.4-0.5 and a responder rejection hazard increasing as the offered share falls below ~0.2 (inequity-aversion / negative-reciprocity correction).

payload.formal_model: Time-dependent concession: offer(t) = initial + (reservation - initial) * (min(1, t/deadline))^(1/beta). beta<1 => Boulware (tough, concede late); beta=1 => linear; beta>1 => Conceder (concede early). Agreement when the two agents' offers cross. Faratin also defines resource-dependent (concede faster as time/resources deplete) and behavior-dependent (imitate opponent's concessions) tactic families. This is a prescriptive/engineering model of a bargaining trajectory, not an estimated law of human concession.

payload.formal_model: Three linked structural mechanisms of costly persistence: (1) Discounted-delay (Rubinstein): payoff of agreement at round t is delta^t * share; complete info => immediate deal, incomplete info => screening delay. (2) War of attrition (Maynard Smith): cost accrues linearly with time persisted; each side chooses a stopping time; symmetric mixed-strategy equilibrium has memoryless (exponential) concession hazard with mean tied to prize value V, dissipating rents. (3) Dollar-auction / all-pay escalation (Shubik): both leading bidders pay; the unique subgame structure has no interior stopping point below V, producing bids exceeding the prize (escalation/entrapment). Common core: sunk costs + second-mover loss => persistence beyond the myopic optimum.

Register as structural with unit tests on the equilibrium properties.

payload.formal_model: Two structural pieces. (1) Minimal winning coalition (Riker size principle): among coalitions whose summed weight/seats >= quota, rational side-payment bargaining selects a MINIMAL one (no dispensable member); registry uses a greedy MWC by descending support share. (2) Power indices on the weighted voting game [q; w_1..w_n]: Banzhaf index beta_i = (number of coalitions in which i is critical) / (total critical swings); Shapley-Shubik phi_i = (orderings in which i is pivotal)/n!. Both are EXACT combinatorial functions of (weights, quota) - no free parameters, no estimation. Power != weight (a large bloc can have zero pivotality; Banzhaf's Nassau County result).

payload.formal_model: Two layers. (1) Cooperative-game STABILITY: given a characteristic function v(S) over coalitions, the CORE is the set of payoff vectors x with sum(x)=v(N) and sum_{i in S} x_i >= v(S) for all S (no subcoalition can profitably defect); when the core is empty or non-unique the NUCLEOLUS (Schmeidler) selects the allocation minimizing the lexicographically ordered vector of maximal excesses e(S,x)=v(S)-sum_{i in S}x_i - the most 'defection-resistant' split. (2) Empirical payoff/stability regularity: Gamson's law - realized payoff share s_i ~= seat/resource contribution share r_i (near-proportional, slope just under 1 with small-party bonus); large deviations from proportionality predict instability and portfolio renegotiation. Defection at the individual level (legislators leaving the party-line coalition) is modeled as party pressure vs member ideal-point pull (Snyder-Groseclose).

payload.formal_model: Attraction update A_i^j(t) = [phi*N(t-1)*A_i^j(t-1) + (delta + (1-delta)*I(j=chosen))*payoff_i^j(t)] / N(t), with experience weight N(t) = rho*N(t-1) + 1; choice via logit P_i^j = exp(lambda*A_i^j)/sum_k exp(lambda*A_i^k). Nests cumulative reinforcement (delta=0, rho=0) and weighted fictitious play / belief learning (delta=1, rho=phi). Parameters: phi (decay of prior attractions), delta (foregone-payoff weight), rho (decay of experience weight), kappa (cumulative vs averaging), lambda (logit precision), N(0) (initial experience weight).
payload.local_refit_feasible: Limited. EWA dynamics require repeated-play panels with round-by-round realized AND foregone payoffs. In-repo MobLab/BehaviorBench economic games (dictator, ultimatum, trust, public goods, guessing) are largely one-shot cross-sections, so the (phi,delta,rho,N0) dynamics are not identifiable without a multi-round game panel (a repeated p-beauty/guessing panel would be the only candidate, if round-level data exist).

payload.formal_model: Logit QRE: each player's choice probability P_i(a) = exp(lambda * EU_i(a; P))/sum_b exp(lambda * EU_i(b; P)), a fixed point in which expected utilities are computed against the equilibrium mixed profile. As lambda->0 choices are uniform; as lambda->infinity QRE converges to Nash. The single-agent 'logit choice' reduction takes expected utilities as given (no equilibrium fixed point).

payload.formal_model: Roth-Erev propensity model: q_nj(t+1) = (1-phi)*q_nj(t) + E_k(j, R(x)), choice P = q_nj / sum_k q_nk, with reinforcement R(x)=payoff - x_min, forgetting/recency parameter phi, and a 'three-step' generalization/experimentation function E_k with parameter epsilon that spreads reinforcement to similar strategies. Basic 1-parameter model has only the initial-propensity strength s(1). A 4-parameter FP-like generalization adds a foregone-info weight d, a maximization parameter m, and a habit/past-experience weight h. Generic value form: Q <- Q + alpha*(r-Q).

payload.formal_model: Sequential search with a reservation utility u*: the agent examines options in sequence, accumulating a consideration set, and STOPS as soon as an option with utility >= u* is encountered (Caplin-Dean-Martin 'Condition 1'). The reservation level is fixed within a standard-choice environment and responds to the environment (rising with set size and object complexity). Simon (1955) is the origin: choose the first option that meets an aspiration level.
payload.local_refit_feasible: No. In-repo datasets record final choices/outcomes but NOT the choice process (which options were examined, in what order, with provisional switches). Reservation utilities and search order are therefore not identifiable from BehaviorBench, Upworthy, StackExchange, CMV, or OmniBehavior.

payload.formal_model: Utility U_i(a) = V(pi_i(a)) + gamma_i * N(a), where pi_i(a) is the monetary payoff of action a, N(a) in [-1,+1] is the elicited social-appropriateness rating of a (incentive-compatibly elicited via a coordination game), and gamma_i >= 0 is heterogeneous norm-sensitivity. Choice maximizes (or is logit over) U. Appropriateness is elicited on a 4-point scale mapped to {-1 very inappropriate, -1/3 somewhat inappropriate, +1/3 somewhat appropriate, +1 very appropriate}.

Contrast rules: random seeding; in-degree (highest-degree) seeding. Coupled to a diffusion model, cascade_size(nomination) > cascade_size(random) ~= cascade_size(in-degree) in the Kim RCT.

payload.formal_model: HK: o_i(t+1) = mean{ o_j : |o_j - o_i| <= epsilon }. payload.formal_model: Direct dyadic reciprocity. Continuous edge update: e_{t+1}=clip(e_t + rate*(v_other - baseline), 0, 1), where v_other is the signed kindness of the counterpart's last action relative to a fairness/expectation baseline (positive reciprocity for kind acts, negative reciprocity/retaliation for hostile acts). Trust-game operationalization (Berg): investor sends s in [0,E]; experimenter triples it (3s); trustee returns r in [0,3s]; reciprocity = dr/ds > 0. Registry callable: reciprocity_update(edge_strength, other_action_value, rate=0.15, baseline=0.0).

payload.formal_model: Kim et al. 2004 (PRECISE STRUCTURE): trust repair is NOT a single scalar recovery; it depends on the INTERACTION of violation type x response. (a) COMPETENCE-based violation -> APOLOGY (accepts blame + signals corrigibility) repairs trust better than DENIAL. (b) INTEGRITY-based violation -> DENIAL repairs trust better than APOLOGY, because admitting an integrity lapse is highly diagnostic of enduring bad character (an integrity apology can be worse than the violation). (c) With subsequent evidence of GUILT, apology > denial; with evidence of INNOCENCE, denial > apology. The violation-type x response effect on trusting intentions is mediated by trusting BELIEFS. Schweitzer: deception-based harm is largely irreversible. The registry trust_update() 'repaired' branch (T_{t+1}=T_t + repair*(1-T_t), single scalar) does NOT represent this 2x2 interaction and is a placeholder.

Keep the scalar only as a degenerate fallback. Structural until experimental parameters are licensed.

payload.formal_model: Indirect reciprocity via image scoring (Nowak-Sigmund): each agent has an image score k (e.g., integer in [-5,+5]); helping raises the donor's score by 1, refusing lowers it (bounded). Discriminator strategy: help recipient iff k_recipient >= own threshold. Cooperation via indirect reciprocity is favored when the probability q of knowing a recipient's score exceeds the cost/benefit ratio: q > c/b. Online systems (Resnick) aggregate feedback into a reputation score that shifts counterparties' willingness to transact/pay.

payload.formal_model: Tie decay: relationship strength / contact probability declines over time absent investment, typically modeled as a hazard/exponential survival of the tie in time-since-last-contact. Burt: decay rate depends on structure — embedded/homophilous/high-status/strong-prior ties decay slowly; bridges decay fast early (liability of newness) but, conditional on surviving early years, then persist. Roberts-Dunbar: emotional closeness E(t) decays toward a floor when contact frequency f and shared activities a fall; kin ties have a lower maintenance cost (shallower decay) than friendships. No canonical closed-form/parametric rate is extracted here.

Needs code_ref + tests to reach 'implemented'.
